name: Gaia
description: ESA's stellar cartographer at L2. Mapping 1.8 billion stars — measuring positions to 7 microarcseconds. Has measured distances to stars 30,000 light-years away with the precision of a human hair's width at 1,000 km distance. The most detailed map of the Milky Way ever made.
scale_fact: Gaia sits alongside JWST at L2. The Lagrange point is valuable real estate — gravitationally stable, thermally stable, permanently facing away from the Sun.
